name: Ampere Audit Style Guide
content:
1. Tone: The tone should be formal, objective, and evidence-based. Avoid subjective adjectives like "excellent" or "terrible".
        2. Formatting: Use clear headings and bullet points for lists of evidence.
        3. Citations: Always reference the specific document name and section number when stating a fact (e.g., "As stated in the PDD, Section 3.1...").
        4. Terminology: Use "Project Proponent" instead of "Client". Use "VVB" instead of "Auditor" in formal sections.

name: Example CAR for Monitoring
Example Report Introduction
content: This report summarizes the independent validation of the 'Rural Solar Power Initiative' project. The project's primary objective is the installation and operation of a 20 MW solar photovoltaic power plant in the province of Example-ville. The validation was conducted against the requirements of the Verified Carbon Standard (VCS), Version 4.1. The assessment covered a review of the Project Description (PD), monitoring plan, and supporting documentation provided by the project proponent, Future Energy Corp.

name: Example Response to Client Finding
content: Thank you for your query regarding Finding CAR-02. We acknowledge the additional data provided. Our team has reviewed the revised emission calculations and can confirm that they now align with the methodological requirements. We recommend updating Section 6.3 of the Project Document to reflect this change. The finding will be officially closed upon receipt of the revised PD. Please let us know if you require any further clarification.
